reasoning:
The volume of protests in Morocco is overwhelmingly driven by the weekly Friday demonstrations organized by the 'Moroccan Commission for the Support of Ummah Causes' opposing the Gaza War and Israeli normalization. These occur every Friday and reliably produce a high volume of separate events (often >100 demonstrations reported by organizers, realistically resulting in 50-80 ACLED events per Friday). The 360-day average leading up to June 8, 2025, is approximately 260.33 protests per 30-day period. Because this average averages out the number of Fridays per 30 days (~4.28 Fridays), any strict 30-day window that contains 5 Fridays has a strong structural advantage to exceed the average, whereas windows with only 4 Fridays are at a structural disadvantage unless protest momentum actually increases. 

Analyzing the resolution dates:
- 2025-06-15 (May 16 - June 14 window): Exactly 5 Fridays (May 16, 23, 30, June 6, 13). Combined with recent May 2025 articles confirming immense ongoing Friday protests (~110 events claimed recently), this window is very likely to exceed 260.33. P=0.70.
- 2025-07-08 (June 8 - July 7 window): Exactly 4 Fridays (June 13, 20, 27, July 4). Additionally, this is deeper into the summer heat (July), which historically suppresses protest turnout slightly. With only 4 Fridays, it is likely to fall short of the 260.33 average. P=0.40.
- 2025-09-06 (August 7 - Sept 5 window): Exactly 5 Fridays (Aug 8, 15, 22, 29, Sept 5). The structural bump of a 5-Friday window acts as a strong upward factor, but because the forecast horizon is further out, there is greater uncertainty around the persistence of the Gaza protests by late summer. P=0.55.
- 2025-12-05 (Nov 5 - Dec 4 window): Exactly 4 Fridays (Nov 7, 14, 21, 28). Lacking the 5th Friday bump, and at a 6-month horizon where the baseline weekly rate of Friday protests is likely to have decayed from earlier peaks, the chance to exceed 260.33 falls noticeably. P=0.35.
